---
applyTo: design-artifacts/**
---

# Design artifacts

- All documentation related to the product should be stored under `design-artifacts/`.

The `design-artifacts/` folder follows the BMAD method pipeline. Each subfolder corresponds to a phase:

- `A-Product-Brief/` — Product brief produced by the `bmad-create-product-brief` or `bmad-product-brief-preview` skill. Contains: project context, problem statement, goals, target users, success metrics, and constraints. Typical file: `product-brief.md`. May also include research reports (domain, market, technical) generated by the `bmad-domain-research`, `bmad-market-research`, and `bmad-technical-research` skills.

- `B-Trigger-Map/` — Trigger map produced by the `bmad-wds-trigger-mapping` workflow. Maps business goals to user psychology: user triggers, motivators, and desired outcomes. Typical file: `trigger-map.md`.

- `C-UX-Scenarios/` — UX scenario outlines produced by the `bmad-wds-scenarios` workflow from the trigger map. Describes user flows as structured micro-steps. One file per scenario (e.g., `scenario-<name>.md`).

- `D-Design-System/` — Design system definitions produced by the `bmad-wds-design-system` workflows. Contains component specifications and design tokens (colors, typography, spacing). Files follow the WDS format.

- `E-PRD/` — Product Requirements Document produced by the `bmad-create-prd` skill. Contains functional requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria. Typical file: `prd.md`. The `Design-Deliveries/` subfolder holds page and UI specifications produced by the `bmad-wds-ux-design` workflow.

- `F-Testing/` — Test architecture and strategy documents produced by the `tea` module workflows (`bmad-testarch-test-design`, `bmad-testarch-atdd`, `bmad-testarch-trace`, `bmad-testarch-ci`, etc.). Contains test plans, ATDD specs, traceability matrices, and CI/CD quality gate definitions.

- `G-Product-Development/` — Implementation planning artifacts produced by the `bmm` implementation workflows. Contains architecture decisions (`bmad-create-architecture`), epics and stories (`bmad-create-epics-and-stories`, `bmad-create-story`), and sprint plans (`bmad-sprint-planning`).
